content.thinking:
Now I can see the issues clearly:

1. `/app` vhost: `ops-user` has full permissions (wrong), but `app-user` has NO permissions (missing)
2. `/ops` vhost: `ops-user` has NO permissions (missing), and `app-user` has no permissions either

The required end state:
- `app-user` has full configure/write/read permissions on `/app`, and NO permissions on `/ops`
- `ops-user` has full configure/write/read permissions on `/ops`, and NO permissions on `/app`
So I need to remove `ops-user` from `/app`, grant `app-user` full permissions on `/app`, and grant `ops-user` full permissions on `/ops`. I should also look at what the client deployments are using for their connection credentials to make sure the changes align with how they're connecting.

content.text:
The situation is clear:
- `/app` vhost: `ops-user` has full access (wrong), `app-user` has no access (missing)
- `/ops` vhost: `ops-user` has no access (missing), `app-user` has no access either

Let me also check the client deployments to understand connection details.

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 24); do
  echo "--- attempt $i ---"
  kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-dr-security-e2e-advers-58071ab9-cluster-a get pods --no-headers 2>&1 | grep -E "app-client|ops-client"
  STATUS=$(kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-dr-security-e2e-advers-58071ab9-cluster-a get pods --no-headers 2>&1 | grep -E "app-client|ops-client" | awk '{print $3}')
  if echo "$STATUS" | grep -qv "Running"; then
    sleep 5
  else
    echo "Both clients are Running!"
    break
  fi
done

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  echo "--- attempt $i ---"
  kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-dr-security-e2e-advers-58071ab9-cluster-a get pods --no-headers 2>&1 | grep -E "app-client|ops-client"
  APP=$(kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-dr-security-e2e-advers-58071ab9-cluster-a get pod -l app=app-client --no-headers 2>&1 | awk '{print $3}')
  OPS=$(kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-dr-security-e2e-advers-58071ab9-cluster-a get pod -l app=ops-client --no-headers 2>&1 | awk '{print $3}')
  if [ "$APP" = "Running" ] && [ "$OPS" = "Running" ]; then
    echo "Both clients are Running!"
    break
  fi
  sleep 5
done

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  annotations:
    deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: "1"
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"apps/v1","kind":"Deployment","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"app-client","namespace":"karma-rabbitmq-dr-security-e2e-advers-58071ab9-cluster-a"},"spec":{"replicas":1,"selector":{"matchLabels":{"app":"app-client"}},"template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"app-client"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["/bin/sh","-c","set -e\nbase=\"http://rabbitmq:15672/api\"\nvhost=\"%2Fapp\"\nqueue=\"app-queue\"\nauth=\"${APP_USER}:${APP_PASS}\"\nfor i in $(seq 1 60); do\n  if curl -s -u \"$auth\" \"$base/vhosts\" \u003e/dev/null; then\n    break\n  fi\n  sleep 2\ndone\npayload='{\"durable\":true,\"auto_delete\":false,\"arguments\":{\"x-queue-type\":\"classic\"}}'\ncode=$(curl -s -o /tmp/resp -w \"%{http_code}\" -u \"$auth\" \\\n  -H \"content-type: application/json\" \\\n  -XPUT \"$base/queues/${vhost}/${queue}\" -d \"$payload\")\nif [ \"$code\" -ge 300 ]; then\n  echo \"app-client: failed to declare queue in /app: $(cat /tmp/resp)\"\n  exit 1\nfi\ni=0\nwhile true; do\n  body='{\"properties\":{\"delivery_mode\":2},\"routing_key\":\"'\"${queue}\"'\",\"payload\":\"app-msg-'\"${i}\"'\",\"payload_encoding\":\"string\"}'\n  code=$(curl -s -o /tmp/pubresp -w \"%{http_code}\" -u \"$auth\" \\\n    -H \"content-type: application/json\" \\\n    -XPOST \"$base/exchanges/${vhost}/amq.default/publish\" -d \"$body\")\n  if [ \"$code\" -ne 200 ]; then\n    echo \"app-client: publish failed: $(cat /tmp/pubresp)\"\n    exit 1\n  fi\n  i=$((i+1))\n  sleep 2\ndone\n"],"env":[{"name":"APP_USER","valueFrom":{"secretKeyRef":{"key":"username","name":"rabbitmq-app"}}},{"name":"APP_PASS","valueFrom":{"secretKeyRef":{"key":"password","name":"rabbitmq-app"}}}],"image":"curlimages/curl:8.6.0","name":"client"}]}}}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-06-23T12:47:39Z"
  generation: 1
  name: app-client
  namespace: karma-rabbitmq-dr-security-e2e-advers-58071ab9-cluster-a
  resourceVersion: "871630"
  uid: 0abb0157-85c0-4eee-9bad-22b82094a51e
spec:
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 600
  replicas: 1
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: app-client
  strategy:
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 25%
      maxUnavailable: 25%
    type: RollingUpdate
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: app-client
    spec:
      containers:
      - command:
        - /bin/sh
        - -c
        - |
          set -e
          base="http://rabbitmq:15672/api"
          vhost="%2Fapp"
          queue="app-queue"
          auth="${APP_USER}:${APP_PASS}"
          for i in $(seq 1 60); do
            if curl -s -u "$auth" "$base/vhosts" >/dev/null; then
              break
            fi
            sleep 2
          done
          payload='{"durable":true,"auto_delete":false,"arguments":{"x-queue-type":"classic"}}'
          code=$(curl -s -o /tmp/resp -w "%{http_code}" -u "$auth" \
            -H "content-type: application/json" \
            -XPUT "$base/queues/${vhost}/${queue}" -d "$payload")
          if [ "$code" -ge 300 ]; then
            echo "app-client: failed to declare queue in /app: $(cat /tmp/resp)"
            exit 1
          fi
          i=0
          while true; do
            body='{"properties":{"delivery_mode":2},"routing_key":"'"${queue}"'","payload":"app-msg-'"${i}"'","payload_encoding":"string"}'
            code=$(curl -s -o /tmp/pubresp -w "%{http_code}" -u "$auth" \
              -H "content-type: application/json" \
              -XPOST "$base/exchanges/${vhost}/amq.default/publish" -d "$body")
            if [ "$code" -ne 200 ]; then
              echo "app-client: publish failed: $(cat /tmp/pubresp)"
              exit 1
            fi
            i=$((i+1))
            sleep 2
          done
        env:
        - name: APP_USER
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              key: username
              name: rabbitmq-app
        - name: APP_PASS
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              key: password
              name: rabbitmq-app
        image: curlimages/curl:8.6.0
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: client
        resources: {}
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30

content.thinking:
The app-client is failing because there's already an `app-queue` queue in the `/app` vhost that was declared with `x-queue-mode: lazy`, but the app-client is trying to redeclare it with `x-queue-type: classic` and no `x-queue-mode`. This is a queue property mismatch - `inequivalent arg 'x-queue-mode'`.

The existing queue `app-queue` was created with `x-queue-mode: lazy` (classic queue with lazy mode), but the app-client is trying to declare it with `x-queue-type: classic` but without the `x-queue-mode: lazy` argument.
I need to delete the existing `app-queue` from the `/app` vhost so the app-client can recreate it with the correct parameters. I'll use the RabbitMQ API to remove it and check the current queue configuration first.

content.text: The issue is a queue argument mismatch: `app-queue` already exists in `/app` with `x-queue-mode: lazy`, but the app-client tries to declare it with just `x-queue-type: classic` (no lazy mode). I need to delete the existing queue so the client can re-declare it correctly.

kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-dr-security-e2e-advers-58071ab9-cluster-a get secret rabbitmq-admin -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' 2>/dev/null | base64 -d; echo

content.thinking:
The existing `app-queue` has both `x-queue-mode: lazy` and `x-queue-type: classic`, but the app-client is trying to declare it with only `x-queue-type: classic` (without `x-queue-mode: lazy`). This causes the "inequivalent arg" error.

I need to delete the existing queue so the app-client can create it fresh. I'll use the RabbitMQ HTTP API to delete the queue.

content.text: The existing `app-queue` was declared with `x-queue-mode: lazy` but the app-client declares it without that argument. I'll delete the queue so the client can recreate it cleanly.

echo "=== All pods ==="
kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-dr-security-e2e-advers-58071ab9-cluster-a get pods 2>&1

echo ""
echo "=== /app vhost permissions ==="
kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-dr-security-e2e-advers-58071ab9-cluster-a exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_permissions -p /app 2>&1

echo ""
echo "=== /ops vhost permissions ==="
kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-dr-security-e2e-advers-58071ab9-cluster-a exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_permissions -p /ops 2>&1

echo ""
echo "=== RabbitMQ cluster status ==="
kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-dr-security-e2e-advers-58071ab9-cluster-a exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl cluster_status 2>&1 | head -20
